concepts.finding: Unified embedding model: two-tower architecture with triplet loss (margin m critical - 5-10% recall variance). Query encoder incorporates text (char n-grams + word n-grams), location features (+2.20% recall), social embedding features (+1.77% recall). Searcher context is integral - not just text.
concepts.implication: Intent/query understanding must incorporate user context (location, social graph, demographics), not just query text. Embedding-based retrieval is a recall optimization problem first, precision optimization second.
concepts.open_question: What contextual signals are available and relevant for keyword clustering without user-level data?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Adding location and domain-specific features to keyword embeddings improves cluster purity over text-only embeddings.

concepts.finding: Training data findings: random negatives outperform non-click impressions as negatives (55% recall regression with non-click impressions). Click as positive and impression as positive perform equally. Hard mining strategies improve model's ability to differentiate similar results.
concepts.implication: For pairwise/triplet training, random negatives are often better than impression-based negatives because they better approximate the real retrieval distribution. Hard negatives matter for fine-grained differentiation.
concepts.open_question: For keyword clustering, should negatives be random keywords or carefully chosen near-neighbor keywords?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Using 'hard' negatives (keywords from adjacent intent categories) in triplet loss produces better cluster separation than random negatives.
